name: Colonial Buckle Shoes
description: Sturdy black leather shoes with brass buckles, suitable for a colonial clerk, tradesman, or town resident in the Atlantic world.

name: Merchant Loafers
description: Worn leather loafers fit for a dockside merchant or shopkeeper, practical for long hours on cobbled streets and countinghouse floors.

name: Naval Dress Shoes
description: Refined dark leather shoes styled for an officer or gentleman attached to Atlantic trade and naval service, polished for formal occasions.

name: Frontier Work Boots
description: Heavy brown work boots built for rough roads, muddy crossings, and daily labor at the edge of settlement.

name: Beaded Moccasins
description: Soft leather moccasins with beadwork and fringe, inspired by Indigenous craftsmanship and well suited for travel over uneven ground.

name: Riding Boots
description: Tall leather boots made for horseback travel, favored by riders, surveyors, and travelers moving between ports and inland settlements.

name: Polished Monk Straps
description: Well-kept brown strap shoes with a polished finish, fitting for a prosperous merchant, lawyer, or educated gentleman in town.

name: Woven Moccasins
description: Lightweight woven leather shoes that suggest hand-crafted frontier wear, comfortable for travel and everyday movement.

name: Weathered Deck Shoes
description: Sea-worn leather shoes suited to sailors and harbor workers, practical on wet planks, docks, and shipboard decks.

name: Fur-Lined Winter Boots
description: Thick leather boots lined with fur for cold-weather travel, ideal for northern colonies, wilderness routes, and winter expeditions.
